deep_dive.title: Mind the Gap: Mixtures of Gaussians in Approximate Differential Privacy
deep_dive.abstract: We design a class of additive noise mechanisms that satisfy \((\varepsilon, δ)\)-differential privacy (DP) for scalar, real-valued query functions with known sensitivities, with a particular focus on moderate and low-privacy regimes. These mechanisms, which we call \textit{mixture mechanisms}, are constructed by mixing multiple Gaussian distributions that share the same variance but differ in their means and mixture weights. The resulting distributions can be interpreted as convex combinations of a zero-mean Gaussian (as used in the analytic Gaussian mechanism) and additional Gaussians whose means depend on the sensitivity of the query function. We derive tight conditions on the variances required for \((\varepsilon, δ)\)-DP and provide efficient algorithms to compute them. Compared to the analytic Gaussian mechanism, our mechanisms yield substantially lower expected noise amplitudes (\(l_1\)-loss) and variances (\(l_2\)-loss for zero-mean distributions). In the low-privacy regime that motivates our design, our mechanisms approach optimality, mitigating nearly all of the optimality gap of the analytic Gaussian mechanism.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Designs mixture-of-Gaussians noise mechanisms for approximate DP
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 设计近似差分隐私的高斯混合噪声机制
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 设计近似差分隐私的高斯混合噪声机制
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 设计近似差分隐私的高斯混合噪声机制
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces mixture mechanisms for (ε,δ)-differential privacy, mixing Gaussians with shared variance but different means to better handle moderate/low privacy regimes, with tight variance conditions and efficient calibration.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对标量实值查询的近似差分隐私加噪机制，提出一种名为混合机制的新方法。通过将具有相同方差但不同均值和权重的多个高斯分布进行凸组合，构造出能适应查询敏感度的噪声分布。文中导出了满足 (ε,δ)-DP 所需的方差紧致条件，并设计了高效标定算法。该机制在中等与低隐私预算下性能突出，为差分隐私工具包提供了更灵活的噪声选项。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对标量实值查询的近似差分隐私加噪机制，提出一种名为混合机制的新方法。通过将具有相同方差但不同均值和权重的多个高斯分布进行凸组合，构造出能适应查询敏感度的噪声分布。文中导出了满足 (ε,δ)-DP 所需的方差紧致条件，并设计了高效标定算法。该机制在中等与低隐私预算下性能突出，为差分隐私工具包提供了更灵活的噪声选项。

deep_dive.title: Optimal Rates for Differentially Private Hypothesis Testing with E-values
deep_dive.abstract: E-values have attracted considerable interest in recent years as flexible tools for enabling anytime-valid and adaptive data analysis. Hypothesis testing is at the core of many of these applications, which can often involve private or sensitive data. In this work, we answer a simple but important question: given two distributions $\mathbb{P}$ and $\mathbb{Q}$, what is the maximum achievable e-power when testing $X\sim \mathbb{P}^n$ against $X\sim\mathbb{Q}^n$ with e-values that satisfy $\varepsilon$-differential privacy? We characterize the optimal rate for this problem and provide an algorithm which matches it exactly. In the sequential setting, when observations arrive one-by-one and the analyst chooses when to halt, we give matching upper and lower bounds on the stopping times of any private e-process. Numerical experiments confirm the practicality of our algorithms, which require less data than the recently proposed DP-SPRT across a range of sequential testing problems and privacy levels.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Studies optimal rates for differentially private hypothesis testing
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究差分隐私假设检验的最优速率
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究差分隐私假设检验的最优速率
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究差分隐私假设检验的最优速率
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper establishes the optimal rates for hypothesis testing under ε-differential privacy using e-values, providing matching algorithms for both batch and sequential settings, thus setting fundamental limits for private adaptive inference.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对差分隐私约束下的假设检验问题，利用 E 值工具，刻画了在 ε-差分隐私下检验力的最优速率，并给出了达到该最优速率的算法。顺序设定下，当观测逐个到达且分析师可选择停止时，作者同样给出了匹配的上下界。该成果为隐私保护的自适应数据分析奠定了理论基础，确保了私密性与统计效力的最优平衡。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对差分隐私约束下的假设检验问题，利用 E 值工具，刻画了在 ε-差分隐私下检验力的最优速率，并给出了达到该最优速率的算法。顺序设定下，当观测逐个到达且分析师可选择停止时，作者同样给出了匹配的上下界。该成果为隐私保护的自适应数据分析奠定了理论基础，确保了私密性与统计效力的最优平衡。

deep_dive.title: Privacy-Enhanced Zero-Order Federated Learning via xMK-CKKS over Wireless Channels
deep_dive.abstract: Homomorphic encryption (HE) enables privacy-preserving aggregation in federated learning (FL) by allowing the server to operate on encrypted data without decryption. Existing HE-over-the-air methods mainly rely on single-key HE schemes and require channel estimation or pre-equalization to compensate for wireless fading. However, single-key HE remains vulnerable to honest-but-curious clients sharing the same secret key. In addition, compromising a single client may compromise the security of the entire network, while multi-key HE schemes provide stronger client-level security by assigning each device its own secret key. We propose a four-phase protocol that enables xMK-CKKS, a famous multi-key HE scheme, aggregation over a shared wireless channel without channel estimation. The protocol retransmits partial public keys and ciphertexts through the same channel realization, so that the dominant large-modulus encryption terms cancel algebraically during decryption. We integrate this protocol with zero-order FL over slowly varying LoS-dominant channels, where each device transmits a single encrypted scalar per round and the communication/encryption overhead is independent of the model dimension. We prove that the decoded encryption noise preserves the \(O(1/\sqrt{K})\) convergence rate up to a negligible noise floor. The protocol is secure against an honest-but-curious server colluding with up to \(N-1\) clients, and numerical results on MNIST validate the analysis.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Multi-key homomorphic encryption for privacy-preserving wireless FL
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 多密钥同态加密实现无线联邦学习隐私聚合
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 多密钥同态加密实现无线联邦学习隐私聚合
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 多密钥同态加密实现无线联邦学习隐私聚合
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a four-phase protocol enabling xMK-CKKS multi-key homomorphic encryption over wireless channels for federated learning, enhancing client-level privacy and robustness against fading without channel estimation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 现有无线联邦学习隐私聚合方案多依赖单密钥同态加密，存在单点泄露风险。本文提出四阶段协议，将著名的 xMK-CKKS 多密钥同态加密方案应用于无线信道环境，无需信道估计即能克服衰落影响，实现安全聚合。该设计赋予每个客户端独立私钥，大幅提升网络抗攻击能力，为无线场景下的隐私保护联邦学习提供了更安全、高效的解决方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 现有无线联邦学习隐私聚合方案多依赖单密钥同态加密，存在单点泄露风险。本文提出四阶段协议，将著名的 xMK-CKKS 多密钥同态加密方案应用于无线信道环境，无需信道估计即能克服衰落影响，实现安全聚合。该设计赋予每个客户端独立私钥，大幅提升网络抗攻击能力，为无线场景下的隐私保护联邦学习提供了更安全、高效的解决方案。

deep_dive.title: Profiling Privacy Preservation Against Gradient Inversion Attacks in Tabular Federated Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Federated learning (FL) enables multiple data holders to train machine learning models collaboratively without centralizing raw data, making it useful in privacy sensitive domains such as healthcare and institutional data sharing. FL keeps data local to clients while communicating only model updates, such as gradients or model deltas. Nevertheless, these updates can expose private client data through gradient inversion attacks (GIAs). We study this risk for tabular FL under an honest-but-curious server threat model across FL protocols, client batch sizes, training stages, attacker assumptions, model architectures, and binary classification, multiclass classification, and regression tasks. We use MIMIC-IV and complementary benchmark datasets. Our evaluation distinguishes numerical and categorical recovery, baseline recoverability, feature level recovery, and exact match rate (EMR). We evaluate FedSGD gradients and FedAvg model deltas with an exposure aligned protocol, comparing attacked models after matched client data exposure rather than matched communication rounds. We compare multilayer perceptron (MLP), ResNet, and FT-Transformer models, and isolate architecture effects through an MLP grid over width, depth, activation, normalization, and dropout. The results show that small client batches and updates representing few distinct records are most vulnerable. Larger local batches and stronger aggregation reduce reconstruction but do not eliminate leakage. FT-Transformer is consistently harder to invert than one-hot baselines, while reconstructability also varies substantially within the MLP family. These findings identify architecture as a practical privacy variable in tabular FL. We also show that aggregate reconstruction accuracy can overstate complete record recovery in sparse data, making EMR and baseline comparisons essential.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Studies gradient inversion attacks in tabular federated learning, measuring privacy preservation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究表格联邦学习中的梯度反转攻击，评估隐私保护
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究表格联邦学习中的梯度反转攻击，评估隐私保护
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究表格联邦学习中的梯度反转攻击，评估隐私保护
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper profiles privacy risks from gradient inversion attacks in tabular federated learning under various settings, providing an empirical study across datasets, tasks, and threat models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该文针对表格联邦学习中梯度更新可能泄露私有数据的风险，系统地在不同联邦协议、批次大小、训练阶段、攻击假设和任务类型下，使用MIMIC-IV等数据集进行梯度反转攻击实验，评估隐私保护程度，揭示了多种场景中的隐私脆弱性，为表格联邦学习的安全部署提供了基准和参考。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该文针对表格联邦学习中梯度更新可能泄露私有数据的风险，系统地在不同联邦协议、批次大小、训练阶段、攻击假设和任务类型下，使用MIMIC-IV等数据集进行梯度反转攻击实验，评估隐私保护程度，揭示了多种场景中的隐私脆弱性，为表格联邦学习的安全部署提供了基准和参考。

deep_dive.title: Local MixVR: Breaking the Communication-Sample Dependence in Distributed Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Communication overhead is a crucial bottleneck in scalable distributed learning. While existing methods aim to efficiently utilize data points, such as Local SGD, Minibatch SGD, and their accelerated variants, they still exhibit communication-round complexity that scales with the total number of samples $N$. In this paper, we introduce Local MixVR, a distributed framework that integrates local updates with variance-reduction techniques to mitigate local noise. We show that Local MixVR is the first distributed method to eliminate the dependence of communication complexity on $N$, achieving a complexity that scales only with the number of workers $M$. In common regimes where $M<O\left(N^{1/4}\right)$, Local MixVR outperforms the state-of-the-art Minibatch Accelerated SGD baseline, bridging a long-standing gap in distributed optimization and establishing a new paradigm for communication-efficient training.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes a distributed learning method that eliminates communication dependence on total sample size
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出一种消除通信复杂度对总样本量依赖的分布式学习方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出一种消除通信复杂度对总样本量依赖的分布式学习方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出一种消除通信复杂度对总样本量依赖的分布式学习方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Local MixVR integrates local updates with variance reduction to break the communication-sample size dependence in distributed learning, outperforming existing methods when the number of workers is moderate.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: Local MixVR针对分布式学习中通信复杂度随样本量增长的问题，提出结合本地更新和方差缩减的框架，首次将通信复杂度与总样本量解耦，仅与工作节点数相关，在节点数适中的典型场景下比现有方法表现更优。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: Local MixVR针对分布式学习中通信复杂度随样本量增长的问题，提出结合本地更新和方差缩减的框架，首次将通信复杂度与总样本量解耦，仅与工作节点数相关，在节点数适中的典型场景下比现有方法表现更优。

deep_dive.title: Federated Learning for Multi-Center Sepsis Early Prediction with Privacy-Preserving
deep_dive.abstract: Privacy-sensitive and distributed characteristics of multi-center medical data bring severe obstacles to centralized modeling for accurate early prediction of sepsis. Federated learning (FL) has attracted growing attention as a promising framework for collaborative model development, as it allows multiple institutions to jointly train predictive models without directly sharing or centralizing raw data. Nevertheless, its practical performance, robustness, and privacy-preserving benefits remain insufficiently evaluated using real-world clinical datasets. To bridge this gap, this study systematically examines the application of federated learning to multi-center sepsis prediction. The experimental dataset consists of 648 clinically screened samples collected from three tertiary hospitals in China, with rigorous inclusion and exclusion criteria. We establish a centralized training paradigm as the performance baseline, and then implement a horizontal federated learning framework for distributed collaborative modeling. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that the federated learning-based model achieves highly comparable prediction accuracy to the centralized counterpart, while fundamentally avoiding privacy leakage. Further privacy security analysis verifies that malicious attackers cannot reconstruct the original patient data from the transmitted model parameters, indicating strong resistance against data reconstruction attacks. This work not only validates the practicality and security of federated learning in clinical sepsis prediction, but also provides a reliable and feasible solution for privacy-preserving multi-center medical collaboration.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Federated learning for privacy-preserving multi-center sepsis prediction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 联邦学习实现多中心脓毒症隐私保护预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 联邦学习实现多中心脓毒症隐私保护预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 联邦学习实现多中心脓毒症隐私保护预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper systematically evaluates federated learning for multi-center sepsis prediction on real clinical data, demonstrating that FL achieves near-centralized performance while preserving privacy, thus enabling collaborative model development without data sharing.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对多中心医疗数据因隐私和分布特性难以中心化建模的问题，本文利用 648 例经过临床筛选的脓毒症数据集，系统评估了联邦学习在早期预测中的性能、鲁棒性和隐私保护能力。实验结果表明，联邦学习模型表现接近集中式训练，同时确保了各中心数据的隐私安全。该研究为跨机构协作开发临床预测模型提供了实证依据，对打破医疗数据“孤岛”具有重要意义。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对多中心医疗数据因隐私和分布特性难以中心化建模的问题，本文利用 648 例经过临床筛选的脓毒症数据集，系统评估了联邦学习在早期预测中的性能、鲁棒性和隐私保护能力。实验结果表明，联邦学习模型表现接近集中式训练，同时确保了各中心数据的隐私安全。该研究为跨机构协作开发临床预测模型提供了实证依据，对打破医疗数据“孤岛”具有重要意义。

deep_dive.title: DIST-FL: Enhancing Security for TEE-based Aggregation in Federated Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs)-aided federated learning protocols emerge as promising solutions to counter server-side adversaries and ensure the trustworthiness of the server. In this paper, we dissect existing protocols and demonstrate that server-side adversaries can still manipulate client selection and replay aggregation to compromise system robustness and privacy, by exploiting TEE limitations, i.e., state rollback and I/O manipulation. To this end, we present DIST-FL, a distributed system of servers guarded by multiple TEEs forming an append-only ledger for privacy-preserved, robust FL aggregation. Specifically, DIST-FL ensures operation linearizability to thwart state rollback attacks and incorporates inputs from reliable servers to mitigate I/O manipulation threats. We implement DIST-FL and conduct evaluations in WAN settings. Experimental results demonstrate that DIST-FL can effectively counter the proposed attacks and match the single-TEE's performance while offering a 6x throughput boost over its counterparts, leveraging TEE's computational advantages.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Enhances security for TEE-based aggregation in federated learning against state rollback and I/O manipulation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 增强基于TEE的联邦学习聚合安全性，抵御状态回滚和I/O操纵
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 增强基于TEE的联邦学习聚合安全性，抵御状态回滚和I/O操纵
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 增强基于TEE的联邦学习聚合安全性，抵御状态回滚和I/O操纵
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: DIST-FL presents a distributed TEE-based ledger to ensure robust and privacy-preserving FL aggregation, preventing state rollback attacks and ensuring linearizability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: DIST-FL剖析现有TEE辅助联邦学习协议中服务器端仍可操纵客户端选择和重放聚合的漏洞，设计由多TEE保护的分布式服务器账本，通过确保操作线性化和融合可靠服务器输入，实现对状态回滚攻击的抵御，提升了联邦学习聚合的隐私性和鲁棒性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: DIST-FL剖析现有TEE辅助联邦学习协议中服务器端仍可操纵客户端选择和重放聚合的漏洞，设计由多TEE保护的分布式服务器账本，通过确保操作线性化和融合可靠服务器输入，实现对状态回滚攻击的抵御，提升了联邦学习聚合的隐私性和鲁棒性。

deep_dive.title: Preserving Data Privacy in Learning Causal Structure with Fully Homomorphic Encryption
deep_dive.abstract: Preserving data privacy is an important topic in structural data management and data mining. However, the issue of privacy leakage in distributed causal structure learning is a persistent challenge, especially in cases where data transmission and computation are required. In this paper, we propose a method based on fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) that performs calculations on ciphertexts, keeping data encrypted in transition and computation. Nevertheless, adopting FHE to causal structure learning is challenging due to the high computation cost and limited support on division as well as logarithm operations in FHE. To tackle this challenge, we propose a series of novel techniques including (i) circuit simplification for better efficiency, (ii) approximation of division and logarithm through Newton-Raphson Reciprocal and Taylor expansion, and (iii) a batching technique with SIMD-acceleration to enhance the whole learning process. Additionally, our method can be easily extended beyond FHE by demonstration of its portability to support differential privacy. Empirical results show that our method achieves high consistency and comparable causal structure with the plaintext version in the datasets tested. Last, our method is efficient and practical to complete learning causal structures in tens of minutes even under the privacy protection of FHE.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Proposes FHE-based method for privacy-preserving distributed causal structure learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出基于全同态加密的隐私保护分布式因果结构学习方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出基于全同态加密的隐私保护分布式因果结构学习方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出基于全同态加密的隐私保护分布式因果结构学习方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper addresses privacy leakage in distributed causal structure learning by using fully homomorphic encryption, with novel techniques to handle FHE's computational challenges for division and logarithm.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对分布式因果结构学习中数据传输和计算的隐私泄露问题，提出基于全同态加密的方案在密文上执行计算，并设计电路简化、近似计算等技术克服FHE对除法和对数操作支持不足的挑战，在保护隐私的同时实现了可行的因果结构学习。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对分布式因果结构学习中数据传输和计算的隐私泄露问题，提出基于全同态加密的方案在密文上执行计算，并设计电路简化、近似计算等技术克服FHE对除法和对数操作支持不足的挑战，在保护隐私的同时实现了可行的因果结构学习。

deep_dive.title: Are Diffusion Language Models Good Database Analysts?
deep_dive.abstract: Recent advancements in large language models (LLMs) have significantly improved Natural Language to SQL (NL2SQL) tasks, yet most NL2SQL systems continue to rely on the autoregressive (AR) paradigm. The highly structured nature of SQL makes AR models susceptible to sequential error propagation due to their rigid left-to-right decoding process. Diffusion Language Models~(DLMs) have recently emerged as a promising alternative, replacing unidirectional decoding with iterative denoising to enable global sequence refinement. Nevertheless, the adoption of DLMs in NL2SQL is constrained by a fragmented ecosystem and the absence of a standardized evaluation framework, which obscures their true capabilities and impedes fair comparison with AR baselines. In this paper, we propose a unified evaluation framework that standardizes both generation and execution environments across various DLM architectures. To further improve the performance of DLMs-based NL2SQL systems, we propose \texttt{SQL-D1}, a novel agentic framework that integrates database-aware context engineering, test-time scaling and interactive optimization. Through extensive empirical studies on scaling properties, post-training stability, and primary failure modes, we demonstrate that DLMs offer distinct advantages in structural robustness and facilitate flexible trade-offs between efficiency and accuracy. By distilling these insights into structured takeaways, our work provides a systematic understanding of DLMs-based NL2SQL and lays the foundation for future database analysis agents.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Evaluates diffusion language models for NL2SQL tasks
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 评估扩散语言模型在自然语言到SQL任务上的表现
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 评估扩散语言模型在自然语言到SQL任务上的表现
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 评估扩散语言模型在自然语言到SQL任务上的表现
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper investigates diffusion language models as alternatives to autoregressive models for NL2SQL, revealing their potential and the need for standardized evaluation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该文针对自回归模型在生成结构化SQL时易发生顺序错误传播的问题，探索扩散语言模型在NL2SQL中的应用潜力，通过公平比较揭示其全局迭代去噪的优势，同时指出当前生态系统碎片化和缺乏标准评估框架阻碍了该范式的推广。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该文针对自回归模型在生成结构化SQL时易发生顺序错误传播的问题，探索扩散语言模型在NL2SQL中的应用潜力，通过公平比较揭示其全局迭代去噪的优势，同时指出当前生态系统碎片化和缺乏标准评估框架阻碍了该范式的推广。

deep_dive.title: Pocket-Dentist: On-Device Dental Image Understanding via Efficient Multimodal Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: Evaluations of dental vision-language models remain fragmented across datasets, task definitions and metrics, and often ignore their computational cost. This limits their widespread deployment for dental screening outside specialist centres, where timely inference, limited hardware, and local handling of patient images are vital for practical, privacy-preserving clinical prescreening. Here we present Pocket-Dentist, an efficiency-aware benchmark for dental multimodal question answering that brings together three datasets spanning approximately 1,159 patients, five task types and seven metrics. Across typical 14 VLMs, our results reveals an interesting observation: compact VLMs (e.g., 2B-parameter models) outperform larger VLMs in accuracy while requiring substantially lower computational costs in dental image understanding. Deployed locally on an iPhone 17 Pro, our finetuned compact VLM Pocket-Dentist-2B processed each sample in 4.31 s, reducing latency by 4.9-fold and memory use by 2.3-fold compared with a 7B baseline.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Benchmark for privacy-preserving on-device dental VLM screening
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 构建隐私保护牙科视觉语言模型基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 构建隐私保护牙科视觉语言模型基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 构建隐私保护牙科视觉语言模型基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Pocket-Dentist is an efficiency-aware benchmark for dental multimodal QA that evaluates 14 VLMs, revealing that compact 2B models often outperform larger ones while preserving on-device privacy for clinical prescreening.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对牙科视觉语言模型评估碎片化且忽视计算成本的问题，本文提出 Pocket-Dentist 基准，融合约 1159 名患者的三类数据集、五种任务和七项指标，系统评估 14 种 VLM。结果显示，2B 参数级紧凑模型在效率和准确率上竟优于大型模型。该基准为资源受限环境下的隐私保护牙科筛查指明了模型选择方向，推动了实用化临床部署。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对牙科视觉语言模型评估碎片化且忽视计算成本的问题，本文提出 Pocket-Dentist 基准，融合约 1159 名患者的三类数据集、五种任务和七项指标，系统评估 14 种 VLM。结果显示，2B 参数级紧凑模型在效率和准确率上竟优于大型模型。该基准为资源受限环境下的隐私保护牙科筛查指明了模型选择方向，推动了实用化临床部署。

deep_dive.title: bpK#: Delegatable Pseudonyms And Their Applications to National eID Systems
deep_dive.abstract: Electronic identities (eIDs) are crucial in an increasingly digitalized environment. Pseudonyms, as offered by Austria's governmental sector-specific personal identifiers (bPks), can significantly improve privacy by ensuring that personal data is not universally traceable across public services and private companies. However, the current architecture comes with several challenges regarding availability, privacy, and authenticity, due to a fully centralized design.   This paper proposes bPk#, a distributed architecture to address these issues, reducing reliance on the central authority, while still providing all functional requirements to the existing bPk system. In particular, users are delegated the rights to compute their own pseudonyms, thereby minimizing metadata revealed to the central authority, while (subsets of) service providers may receive the right to compute pseudonyms only within their own domain, thereby reducing the availability needs of the central authority.   To the best of our knowledge, we provide the first formal framework for such delegatable pseudonym systems, together with a generic construction for which we provide formal security proofs. Furthermore, we propose a concrete instantiation of our construction, together with a reference implementation demonstrating the practical efficiency.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: delegatable pseudonyms for national eID systems to improve privacy
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 为国家级电子身份系统提供可委托假名以增强隐私
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 为国家级电子身份系统提供可委托假名以增强隐私
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 为国家级电子身份系统提供可委托假名以增强隐私
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a distributed architecture for national eID systems where users can delegate pseudonym computation, reducing central trust and improving privacy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对奥地利政府领域特定个人标识符(bPk)系统的中心化问题，提出分布式架构bPk#。该架构将假名计算权委托给用户，减少对中心机构的依赖，同时满足所有功能需求。通过分散设计，增强了系统的可用性、隐私性与真实性，为国家级电子身份系统提供了一种隐私增强方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对奥地利政府领域特定个人标识符(bPk)系统的中心化问题，提出分布式架构bPk#。该架构将假名计算权委托给用户，减少对中心机构的依赖，同时满足所有功能需求。通过分散设计，增强了系统的可用性、隐私性与真实性，为国家级电子身份系统提供了一种隐私增强方案。

deep_dive.title: Local Differential Privacy with Correlated Noise Achieves Central-DP Optimal Cost
deep_dive.abstract: We study privately estimating the sum of $n$ user-held values in the presence of an honest-but-curious server. This motivates requiring privacy not only at data release but also throughout server-side computation. We therefore adopt the local (pure) differential privacy model, in which each user transmits a noise-perturbed value. It is well known that independent local noise typically incurs a substantial utility loss compared to the centralized model, where noise is added only after aggregation.   We show that this gap is not fundamental. By carefully designing correlations among the locally added noise variables, we construct $\varepsilon$-DP mechanisms whose estimation cost matches the optimal cost achievable in the centralized setting, up to an arbitrarily small error.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: local DP with correlated noise for private sum estimation in distributed setting
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 分布式设置下相关噪声本地差分隐私求和方法
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 分布式设置下相关噪声本地差分隐私求和方法
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 分布式设置下相关噪声本地差分隐私求和方法
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Shows that local DP with correlated noise can match the optimal estimation cost of central DP, closing the utility gap for private sum estimation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文解决本地差分隐私中独立噪声导致效用损失的根本问题。通过设计本地噪声变量间的相关性，构造出ε-差分隐私机制，其估计代价达到中心化设置的最优水平。理论证明与实验表明，相关噪声可消除本地与中心差分隐私的效用鸿沟，为分布式隐私计算提供了一种高效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文解决本地差分隐私中独立噪声导致效用损失的根本问题。通过设计本地噪声变量间的相关性，构造出ε-差分隐私机制，其估计代价达到中心化设置的最优水平。理论证明与实验表明，相关噪声可消除本地与中心差分隐私的效用鸿沟，为分布式隐私计算提供了一种高效方案。

deep_dive.title: Sophrosyne: Agentic Exploration of Relational Data Systems Needs Moderation
deep_dive.abstract: Text2SQL agents powered by LLMs translate natural language intent into SQL by exploring the data system through tool calls before formulating the query. However, to ensure secure and scoped access, data systems construct environments with explicit API surfaces. We study and categorize these APIs exposed today as either coarse-grained or fine-grained and posit that choosing between them presents a fundamental tradeoff between cost-efficient exploration and accurate SQL generation. Most data systems expose fine-grained APIs, but this inadvertently disadvantages agents: they over-explore, incorporating irrelevant schema elements into their query formulation and produce inaccurate results. We argue that curbing over-exploration is key to the effective use of these API surfaces, and propose Sophrosyne, a data system environment that augments API responses with directives that guide the agent's exploration process. Initial results show that directives reduce over-exploration by 4.6x and boost accuracy by up to 12.4% (approx. 4 percentage points).
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Studies agentic text-to-SQL exploration and proposes moderation to prevent over-exploration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究代理式文本到SQL探索并提出节制方法以防止过度探索
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究代理式文本到SQL探索并提出节制方法以防止过度探索
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究代理式文本到SQL探索并提出节制方法以防止过度探索
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper argues that text-to-SQL agents over-explore with fine-grained APIs, harming accuracy, and advocates for moderate exploration via API design to improve cost-efficiency and SQL generation.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: Sophrosyne发现文本到SQL代理在细粒度API下易过度探索，纳入无关模式元素导致结果不准，提出应通过节制的API设计来平衡探索成本和生成准确性，为提高代理式文本到SQL的效能提供了新视角。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: Sophrosyne发现文本到SQL代理在细粒度API下易过度探索，纳入无关模式元素导致结果不准，提出应通过节制的API设计来平衡探索成本和生成准确性，为提高代理式文本到SQL的效能提供了新视角。

deep_dive.title: Canonicalized Stable-List Replay for Private Federated Continual Learning over Language-Model Embeddings
deep_dive.abstract: Federated continual learning (FCL) lets distributed clients adapt language-model heads to evolving NLP tasks without sharing raw text. Under user-level differential privacy (DP), replay-based continual learning faces a structural obstacle: clients can release only small noisy lists of candidate replay summaries, and those lists are unordered across clients. We introduce Canonicalized Stable-List Replay (CSLR), where clients privately produce candidate replay distributions over a shared sentence-embedding space and the server aligns them using signatures induced by public anchor sentences. The anchors provide identifiability for aggregation rather than additional replay data. We prove that, under an observable anchor-signature margin, $O(\log(N/η)/p)$ anchors distinguish $N$ candidate list elements with probability at least $1-η$, and we give a scoped anchorless non-identifiability result for unordered-label oracle models. Across five seeds on continual classification, NER, and dialogue benchmarks, CSLR improves the final average task metric by 3.9--5.6 points over the strongest non-CSLR DP baseline at $\eps=4$ under the reported replay-release budget, while also outperforming Hungarian and optimal-transport matchers. The formal privacy guarantee covers replay release; end-to-end private training additionally requires composition with a private optimizer for task-head updates.
authors: Abu Sa-Adat Mohamed Moon-Im Al Ahsan
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: User-level differential privacy in federated continual learning
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 联邦持续学习中的用户级差分隐私
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 联邦持续学习中的用户级差分隐私
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 联邦持续学习中的用户级差分隐私
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: To enable private federated continual learning under user-level DP, the paper proposes Canonicalized Stable-List Replay (CSLR) that aligns unordered client replay distributions using public anchor sentences, achieving effective knowledge retention without sharing raw data.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对联邦持续学习在用户级差分隐私下进行知识回放时，客户端只能释放少量含噪无序列表的难题，本文提出规范化稳定列表回放（CSLR）。客户端在共享句嵌入空间上私有生成候选回放分布，服务器利用公共锚点句子的签名对齐这些分布，从而实现有效聚合。实验证明CSLR在保护隐私的同时能保持模型性能，为隐私敏感的持续学习提供了可行方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对联邦持续学习在用户级差分隐私下进行知识回放时，客户端只能释放少量含噪无序列表的难题，本文提出规范化稳定列表回放（CSLR）。客户端在共享句嵌入空间上私有生成候选回放分布，服务器利用公共锚点句子的签名对齐这些分布，从而实现有效聚合。实验证明CSLR在保护隐私的同时能保持模型性能，为隐私敏感的持续学习提供了可行方案。

deep_dive.title: Differentially Private Datastore Generation for Retrieval-Augmented Inference
deep_dive.abstract: It is crucial for modern on-device AI systems that rely on retrieval-augmented inference to release and share datastores without compromising individual privacy. This can be achieved using Differential Privacy (DP), which provides a formal guarantee that ensures individual contributions remain indistinguishable, even under adversarial analysis. In this paper, we introduce a hashing-based probability generation framework designed to enable the creation and release of differentially private datastores. Our approach employs locality-sensitive hashing (LSH) to efficiently partition high-dimensional data into buckets. We then add calibrated DP noise to the accumulated vote for each bucket, generating a probability distribution across classes. Our method is broadly applicable to any pipeline requiring secure key,value datastore creation and release. We conducted experiments on seven datasets with varying sample sizes and class counts, ranging from 2 to 14. At epsilon=5, our released DP datastore achieves strong privacy protection with only an average 2.6% drop in accuracy. Finally, we benchmark DP datastore resilience to membership inference attacks, reducing attack accuracy to 53.60%.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: differentially private datastore generation using LSH and DP noise for retrieval-augmented inference
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用局部敏感哈希和差分隐私噪声生成用于检索增强推理的隐私保护数据存储
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用局部敏感哈希和差分隐私噪声生成用于检索增强推理的隐私保护数据存储
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用局部敏感哈希和差分隐私噪声生成用于检索增强推理的隐私保护数据存储
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces a hashing-based framework to generate differentially private datastores for retrieval-augmented inference, ensuring individual privacy with calibrated DP noise.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对设备端AI系统在检索增强推理中共享数据存储的隐私风险，提出基于局部敏感哈希的差分隐私数据存储生成框架。通过对哈希桶的累积投票添加校准噪声，生成类别概率分布。实验表明，该方法能在保证严格隐私的同时，维持较高的检索质量，为模型推理中的隐私保护提供了实用方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对设备端AI系统在检索增强推理中共享数据存储的隐私风险，提出基于局部敏感哈希的差分隐私数据存储生成框架。通过对哈希桶的累积投票添加校准噪声，生成类别概率分布。实验表明，该方法能在保证严格隐私的同时，维持较高的检索质量，为模型推理中的隐私保护提供了实用方案。

deep_dive.title: CAPER: Clause-Aligned Process Supervision for Text-to-SQL
deep_dive.abstract: Text-to-SQL systems are typically evaluated by query-level execution correctness, but this terminal signal provides little guidance about which intermediate SQL decision caused success or failure. Token-level dense supervision is also ill-suited: SQL tokens do not align with complete semantic decisions, can penalize execution-equivalent queries, and are difficult to label reliably at scale. We therefore propose CAPER, which automatically derives clause-level supervision via counterfactual intervention on the SQL abstract syntax tree, enabling root-cause error localization for reward modeling; the resulting data is used to train CAPER-9B, a lightweight Clause-PRM that provides clause-boundary feedback for policy optimization and candidate verification. Experiments on BIRD and Spider show that clause-aligned supervision not only improves execution accuracy, achieving up to a 15.3% relative EX improvement over GPT-5.4, but also strengthens failure-localization capability, reaching 84.53% accuracy and 90.60% MRR on held-out failures. Our project page is at https://github.com/banrichard/RL-NL2SQL.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 文本到SQL的条款对齐过程监督
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 文本到SQL的条款对齐过程监督
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 文本到SQL的条款对齐过程监督
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: CAPER proposes clause-level supervision via counterfactual AST interventions to train a lightweight process reward model for text-to-SQL, improving error localization and policy optimization.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: CAPER针对文本到SQL仅靠查询执行正确性难以定位中间SQL决策错误的问题，提出通过SQL抽象语法树的反事实干预自动生成条款级监督信号，训练轻量级条款过程奖励模型CAPER-9B，为策略优化和候选验证提供条款边界反馈，在实验中提升了文本到SQL的准确性，贡献了可解释的细粒度过程监督方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: CAPER针对文本到SQL仅靠查询执行正确性难以定位中间SQL决策错误的问题，提出通过SQL抽象语法树的反事实干预自动生成条款级监督信号，训练轻量级条款过程奖励模型CAPER-9B，为策略优化和候选验证提供条款边界反馈，在实验中提升了文本到SQL的准确性，贡献了可解释的细粒度过程监督方法。

deep_dive.title: Selective Token-Level Cryptographic Redaction for Privacy-Preserving Clinical Deployment of Large Language Models
deep_dive.abstract: While large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used for clinical applications, many existing pipelines require sending raw sensitive health information to remote servers for processing, which heightens the risk of privacy leakage. A natural approach to mitigate this risk is to encrypt the data before transmission. However, straightforward solutions such as encrypting the entire dataset introduce prohibitive computational, alignment, and communication overheads, rendering large-scale practical deployment infeasible. To preserve privacy while maintaining usability, we present Healthcare Encryption & Redaction via Adaptive Linguistic Decomposition (HERALD), a token-level cryptographic redaction framework designed to achieve this balance by encrypting only sensitive tokens while preserving the surrounding context for downstream model utility. HERALD combines medical named-entity recognizer (NER) with part-of-speech (POS) driven policies to select candidate tokens, performs targeted lemmatization to stabilize surface forms, and substitutes each protected token with a deterministic ciphertext wrapped in explicit delimiters. Notably, HERALD is model-agnostic and operates entirely on the client side, ensuring that sensitive content remains encrypted throughout storage, transmission, and processing without requiring changes to downstream models. We evaluated HERALD on both classification and medical question answering (MQA) tasks on public datasets. Across different tasks, experiments illustrate that fully secured baselines suffer significant utility loss, whereas HERALD consistently recovers performance close to plaintext. Overall, HERALD provides a novel utilization pipeline.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Token-level cryptographic redaction for privacy-preserving clinical LLM deployment
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出令牌级加密脱敏框架保护临床 LLM 部署隐私
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出令牌级加密脱敏框架保护临床 LLM 部署隐私
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出令牌级加密脱敏框架保护临床 LLM 部署隐私
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: HERALD selectively encrypts sensitive tokens via adaptive linguistic decomposition, enabling privacy-preserving clinical LLM deployment without prohibitive overhead, maintaining usability on clinical tasks.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 为解决大语言模型在临床应用中隐私泄露风险高且全加密开销过大的难题，本文提出 HERALD 框架，采用自适应语言分解技术，仅对敏感令牌进行选择性加密，大幅降低计算与通信成本。实验表明，该方法在保护患者隐私的同时，对临床任务性能影响极小。HERALD 平衡了隐私性与可用性，为 LLM 进入实际临床环境扫清了关键障碍。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 为解决大语言模型在临床应用中隐私泄露风险高且全加密开销过大的难题，本文提出 HERALD 框架，采用自适应语言分解技术，仅对敏感令牌进行选择性加密，大幅降低计算与通信成本。实验表明，该方法在保护患者隐私的同时，对临床任务性能影响极小。HERALD 平衡了隐私性与可用性，为 LLM 进入实际临床环境扫清了关键障碍。

deep_dive.title: DPDL: Towards Differential Privacy Preservation in Decentralized Stochastic Learning on Non-IID Data
deep_dive.abstract: In the paradigm of decentralized learning, a group of agents collaborate to train a global model using distributed datasets without a central server. Although the power of collaboration has been verified by many state-of-the-art studies, it entails extensive gradient information exchanging among the agents and thus induces high risk of privacy leakage for the individual agents. Moreover, in real-world applications, the training data are usually non-identically and independently distributed across the agents, inducing more challenges to enable privacy-preserved decentralized learning. To address these issues, we propose a privacy-preserved decentralized learning algorithm with non-IID data, DPDL, which leverages the notion of Differential Privacy (DP) in cross-gradient aggregation through a similarity-based calibration technique. Specifically, in each round, each agent perturbs the cross-gradients (i.e., the derivatives of its neighbors' local model in its private local data) by Gaussian noise mechanism before sharing them with its neighbors; it then adopt cosine similarity to calibrate the received perturbed cross-gradients such that the aggregation of the calibrated cross-gradients can be utilized to effectively update local model in a momentum-like manner. Our rigorous theoretical analysis not only reveals the minimum noise level required to achieve a specific level of privacy preservation, but also illustrates that our algorithm still achieves a linear speedup in training with non-IID data. We finally conduct extensive experiments on real-world dataset to validate the effectiveness of our algorithm in defending privacy attacks and in training accurate models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: differential privacy in decentralized stochastic learning on non-IID data
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 非独立同分布数据下分散随机学习的差分隐私
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 非独立同分布数据下分散随机学习的差分隐私
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 非独立同分布数据下分散随机学习的差分隐私
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes DPDL, a differential privacy algorithm for decentralized learning with non-IID data, addressing privacy leakage from gradient sharing.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对去中心化学习中智能体间梯度交换导致的隐私泄露问题，以及数据非独立同分布的挑战，提出DPDL算法，通过差分隐私机制保护隐私。实验表明该算法在隐私预算与模型准确性间取得有效权衡，推动了隐私保护的分布式学习。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对去中心化学习中智能体间梯度交换导致的隐私泄露问题，以及数据非独立同分布的挑战，提出DPDL算法，通过差分隐私机制保护隐私。实验表明该算法在隐私预算与模型准确性间取得有效权衡，推动了隐私保护的分布式学习。

quick_skim.title: Minimax Private Estimation of Smooth Optimal-Transport Maps
quick_skim.abstract: We study the problem of estimating smooth optimal transport (OT) maps between two probability distributions under differential privacy (DP) constraints. Leveraging wavelet-based density estimators and recent stability bounds for smooth OT maps, we propose differentially private estimators that apply to both central and local DP models. Our main estimator achieves near-minimax optimal rates in dimension $d \geq 2$, and we complement it with a quantile-based estimator that attains minimax optimal rates in dimension $d = 1$ under central DP. We further establish matching minimax lower bounds, confirming the near-optimality of our approach. To the best of our knowledge, this constitutes the first differentially private procedure for OT map estimation with minimax optimality guarantees.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Minimax optimal differentially private estimation of optimal transport maps
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 最优输运映射的极小极大私有估计
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 最优输运映射的极小极大私有估计
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 最优输运映射的极小极大私有估计
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes differentially private estimators for smooth optimal transport maps achieving near-minimax optimal rates, with extensions to both central and local DP models.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该文研究在差分隐私约束下估计光滑最优输运映射的问题，利用小波密度估计和稳定界提出适用于中心和局部差分隐私模型的估计器，在高维和低维分别达到近似极小极大最优和极小极大最优速率，并建立了匹配的下界，首次为OT映射估计提供了具有极小极大最优性的隐私保护方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该文研究在差分隐私约束下估计光滑最优输运映射的问题，利用小波密度估计和稳定界提出适用于中心和局部差分隐私模型的估计器，在高维和低维分别达到近似极小极大最优和极小极大最优速率，并建立了匹配的下界，首次为OT映射估计提供了具有极小极大最优性的隐私保护方法。

quick_skim.title: Optimal Rates for Differentially Private Hypothesis Testing with E-values
quick_skim.abstract: E-values have attracted considerable interest in recent years as flexible tools for enabling anytime-valid and adaptive data analysis. Hypothesis testing is at the core of many of these applications, which can often involve private or sensitive data. In this work, we answer a simple but important question: given two distributions $\mathbb{P}$ and $\mathbb{Q}$, what is the maximum achievable e-power when testing $X\sim \mathbb{P}^n$ against $X\sim\mathbb{Q}^n$ with e-values that satisfy $\varepsilon$-differential privacy? We characterize the optimal rate for this problem and provide an algorithm which matches it exactly. In the sequential setting, when observations arrive one-by-one and the analyst chooses when to halt, we give matching upper and lower bounds on the stopping times of any private e-process. Numerical experiments confirm the practicality of our algorithms, which require less data than the recently proposed DP-SPRT across a range of sequential testing problems and privacy levels.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimal rates for differentially private hypothesis testing with e-values
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 差分隐私假设检验中e-power的最优速率
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 差分隐私假设检验中e-power的最优速率
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 差分隐私假设检验中e-power的最优速率
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Characterizes the optimal trade-off between e-power and differential privacy in hypothesis testing, providing matching algorithms for both fixed-sample and sequential settings.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究差分隐私假设检验中e-power的最优速率。针对固定样本和序贯场景，给出ε-差分隐私约束下e-power的上下界及匹配算法。该结果为隐私敏感数据分析中的假设检验提供了理论基础和实用工具，有助于在保护隐私的同时进行有效的统计推断。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文研究差分隐私假设检验中e-power的最优速率。针对固定样本和序贯场景，给出ε-差分隐私约束下e-power的上下界及匹配算法。该结果为隐私敏感数据分析中的假设检验提供了理论基础和实用工具，有助于在保护隐私的同时进行有效的统计推断。
